channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PNP Reminds Officers to Stay Politically Neutral Following ICC Warrant on Dela Rosa
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has reminded its personnel to avoid posting political content online, following the issuance of an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant against former...

item.title: Akume praises Tinubu’s reforms and supports Wadada’s 2027 governorship bid
item.description: Secretary to the Government of the Federation, George Akume, don yarn say President Bola Ahmed Tinubu na prepared and visionary leader wey dey committed to reposition Nigeria through correct economic ...

item.title: FG Reports Over One Million Street Children Returned to School, Nationwide Mapping Underway
item.description: Minister of Education, Tunji Alausa, don yan say the Federal Government don manage to return more than one million children wey dey street back to school for the past 30 months. This na part of the on...

item.title: Joan Friedman and Her Iconic Influence on 1950s Fashion
item.description: Joan Friedman (1931–2014) was one of the most prominent figures of 1950s fashion, epitomizing elegance and sophistication during the era. As a high-fashion model, she worked with renowned photographer...

item.title: Uma Thurman's Iconic Appearances on Vogue Covers
item.description: Uma Thurman has had a distinguished and multifaceted career, not only as a celebrated actress but also as a fashion icon, with numerous appearances on Vogue magazine covers. Her journey began in her t...

item.title: Man jailed for sexually abusing children and attempting to evade justice by feigning disability
item.description: John Siddell, 41, has been sentenced to 15 years in prison with an additional two-year extended licence period for sexually abusing three boys in Leicestershire between 2018 and 2021. His elder brothe...

item.title: 'Filing for Love': K-drama sheds light on corporate life challenges
item.description: The latest K-drama series 'Filing for Love' has caught the attention of viewers for its candid portrayal of the corporate world. Stars Shin Hye-sun and Gong Myung shared that the series aims to reveal...

item.title: Investigations Underway After Multiple Cambridge Exam Papers Leaked Online
item.description: KARACHI: Students and parents are facing increased worry after multiple Cambridge exam papers were allegedly leaked online. The first incident occurred on April 29, when the AS-level Mathematics paper...

item.title: The Life and Art of Tilly Losch: A Jazz Age Icon
item.description: Tilly Losch (1903–1975) was an Austrian-born dancer, actress, and painter whose career embodied the glamour and artistic innovation of the early 20th century. Renowned for her ethereal beauty and flui...

item.title: Tanzania Plans Mineral Processing Facility in Shinyanga to Support Local Miners
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to set up a Mineral Processing Center in Mwakitolyo, Shinyanga Region, aiming to strengthen local participation and add value to minerals extracted in the ...

item.title: Comelec allows 2025 election candidates who lost to be appointed after 1-year ban ends
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has announced that candidates who were unsuccessful in the 2025 elections are now eligible for government appointments, following the conclusion of the one-year p...

item.title: 16 Teams to Showcase Talent at The9jaboy Grassroots Football Tournament in Lagos
item.description: Today, Wednesday, May 13, 2026, a 16-team grassroots football competition don start for Lagos, organised by Nigerian travel brand The9jaboy. This tournament na part of efforts to discover young footba...

item.title: Penélope Cruz Reflects on Her Challenging Debut in 'Jamón Jamón' at Age 17

item.title: Strategies for Managing US-China Rivalry Without Military Conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s recent visit to Beijing highlights the ongoing, unavoidable rivalry between the United States and China, but it does not signal an inevitable slide into war. The central ch...

item.title: US imposes visa restrictions on 13 individuals tied to Indian company over fentanyl-laced pills

item.title: Researchers Discover How Plants Produce the Cancer-Fighting Compound Mitraphylline
item.description: Researchers at the University of British Columbia Okanagan have unveiled the biological process by which certain tropical plants produce mitraphylline, a rare natural compound with potential anti-canc...

item.title: The Life and Work of Irene of New York, Mid-Century Milliner
item.description: Irene of New York, born Irene Stone, was a prominent American milliner whose designs shaped Manhattan fashion from the 1940s through the 1960s. Operating her boutique on East 54th Street, she gained r...

item.title: Former NBA player Jason Collins passes away following brain cancer fight
item.description: Jason Collins, a former NBA player, has sadly passed away after a courageous battle with brain cancer. Known for his career in professional basketball in the United States, Collins played for multiple...

item.title: Shine Rosman Talks About Rising Sexual Harassment in Lagos and Urges Women to Protect Themselves
item.description: Shine Rosman, the Nigerian-Canadian actress wey people sabi for her role for To Kill A Monkey, don talk say sexual harassment dey rise sharply for Lagos, especially for Yaba. She yan say plenty women ...

item.title: Psychological Challenges Faced by Cruise Ship Passengers During Andes Virus Quarantine
item.description: In early April 2026, the MV Hondius departed from Ushuaia, Argentina, and soon after, passengers and crew were exposed to the Andes virus, a type of hantavirus capable of limited human-to-human transm...
